commit.message:
fix(comparator): use safe three-way pattern for uint64_t qsort comparators (#1816)

* fix(comparator): use safe three-way pattern for uint64_t qsort comparators

Replace `a - b` subtraction (which truncates uint64_t to int, producing
wrong results when the difference exceeds INT_MAX or wraps unsigned) with
the safe `(a > b) - (a < b)` pattern in:

- src/graph/graph_delete_edges.c: _edge_cmp NodeID comparisons
- src/graph/tensor/tensor.c: _value_cmp entity ID comparison
- src/value.c: SIValue_Compare T_NODE/T_EDGE entity ID comparison

Closes #1813

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test(comparator): add regression tests for uint64_t overflow in SIValue_Compare

Add unit tests that exercise SIValue_Compare with T_NODE and T_EDGE
entities having large uint64_t IDs (>= 2^32) that trigger the
truncation-to-int overflow bug. Tests verify:

- IDs differing by exactly 2^32 (canonical overflow trigger)
- Antisymmetry: cmp(a,b) and cmp(b,a) have opposite signs
- Equality at large IDs
- IDs differing by 2*2^32
- Near-UINT64_MAX boundary
- Normal small IDs as control

These tests FAIL before the fix (a-b truncation) and PASS after
applying the (a>b)-(a<b) pattern.

Refs #1813

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(test): add __mocks__ stubs for @upstash/ratelimit and @upstash/redis

Vitest resolves vi.mock() targets at module graph time.
@upstash/ratelimit and @upstash/redis are optional runtime-only deps
(dynamic import in _rateLimit.ts) — never added to package.json.
Without stubs the module resolver throws before the mock factory runs,
causing the entire test suite to fail.

Stubs expose the minimal API exercised by _rateLimit.test.ts:
- Ratelimit class with slidingWindow() + limit()
- Redis class (constructor only)

* fix(tsconfig): exclude __mocks__ from tsc compilation

__mocks__/@upstash stubs are Vitest-only artifacts.
tsc picks them up by default (no explicit include) and fails
because @upstash/* types are not in node_modules.
Adding __mocks__ to exclude keeps verify green.

* fix(test): move @upstash stubs to api/__mocks__ (Vitest manual mock convention)

Vitest resolves manual mocks relative to the file under test, not the
project root. __mocks__ at root is a Jest-only convention.
Moving stubs to api/__mocks__ ensures vi.mock('@upstash/ratelimit')
and vi.mock('@upstash/redis') resolve correctly when Vitest runs
api/_rateLimit.test.ts.

chore: fix vulnerabilities and ESM-only dependency issues

- Replace deprecated keycloak-admin with @keycloak/keycloak-admin-client ~26.5.7
  - Uses native dynamic import() via Function() trick to load ESM from CJS output
  - The old keycloak-admin package was unmaintained and brought in critical
    axios vulnerabilities (GHSA-wf5p-g6vw-rhxx, GHSA-jr5f-v2jv-69x6, etc.)
- Remove apollo-server-core and apollo-server-express (abandoned, no fix
  available for GHSA-9q82-xgwf-vj6h; @nestjs/apollo@12 uses @apollo/server)
- Upgrade @angular-devkit/core and @angular-devkit/schematics 20.3.16->20.3.23
  (fixes ajv ReDoS GHSA-2g4f-4pwh-qvx6 and picomatch GHSA-v3rj-xjv7-4jmq)
- Upgrade lerna 9.0.3->9.0.7 (fixes tar path-traversal vulnerabilities)
- Upgrade markdownlint-cli 0.47.0->0.48.0 (fixes smol-toml DoS GHSA-v3rj-xjv7-4jmq)
- Add axios override ^1.15.0 for transitive consumers
- Remaining 14 vulnerabilities all require NestJS v11 upgrade (tracked for
  the next major version branch)

ESM note: @keycloak/keycloak-admin-client is ESM-only. The adapter uses
  new Function('m', 'return import(m)') to perform a native dynamic import()
  at runtime, bypassing TypeScript's CommonJS compilation of import() into
  require(). Type-only import (import type) is used for full type safety
  via KcAdminClient type and KcUser utility type derived from the client API.

fix(TOP/tb): resolve arbiter3 concurrent bit-select race

Root cause: logic [2:0] src_valid is a packed vector. When three
forked threads concurrently write src_valid[0], [1], [2] with
blocking assignments, each does a read-modify-write on the same
variable. In Xcelium, the last writer wins, clearing bits set by
earlier threads. This prevents some FIFOs from receiving their
packets, so pkt_available never reaches 3'b111.

Fix: Change src_valid from packed logic [2:0] to unpacked logic [3]
(same as src_data). Each element is a separate variable, eliminating
the concurrent r-m-w race. Also add 2-cycle settle after fork/join
for packet_count_q to register the last EOC.

TB issue, NOT an RTL bug.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix __proto__ semantics: object literal proto-init, GOPD, and Proxy setPrototypeOf trap

- getOwnPropertyDescriptor: only report __proto__ as own property when
  OWN_DUNDER_PROTO_DATA_KEY is present or it's an accessor; the __proto__
  map entry is the internal [[Prototype]] slot, not an own data property.
- assign_named_property: delegate __proto__ assignment on Proxy receivers
  to Object.setPrototypeOf, which correctly invokes the setPrototypeOf trap.
- InitProperty/InitIndex: when key is __proto__, only clear
  OWN_DUNDER_PROTO_DATA_KEY instead of remove_property_completely, so the
  internal prototype chain entry is preserved.

Fixes 4 cluster-14 tests (__proto__-value-null/obj/non-object,
__proto__-duplicate-computed) and 1 cluster-28 test (set-abrupt).

commit.message:
fix: add window.__CSRF__ pattern for updated Passport HTML (#9)

* fix: add window.__CSRF__ pattern for updated Passport HTML

Yandex Passport now renders the CSRF token as:

    window.__CSRF__ = "value";

instead of the previous `csrf_token` input/JS/JSON formats. The existing
three patterns all match empty strings on the new page, causing
`CsrfExtractionError`.

Add a fourth pattern to `CSRF_PATTERNS` that matches the new
`window.__CSRF__` global assignment. Existing patterns are preserved for
backward compatibility.

- Add `re.compile(r'window\.__CSRF__\s*=\s*\"([^\"]+)\"\')`
  to `CSRF_PATTERNS` in `constants.py`
- Add `tests/fixtures/csrf_window_global.html` fixture
- Extend `test_csrf_patterns` parametrize to cover the new pattern

commit.message:
fix(security): integrate Key Vault for secret management (#92)

* fix(security): add Content-Security-Policy and security headers (fixes #86)

Add a restrictive Content-Security-Policy header to staticwebapp.config.json:
- default-src 'self' — only same-origin by default
- script-src 'self' — no inline scripts
- style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' — needed for Fluent UI runtime styles
- img-src 'self' data: https: — allow data URIs and HTTPS images
- connect-src 'self' https://*.openai.azure.com — allow Azure OpenAI API calls
- font-src 'self' data: — allow embedded fonts
- object-src 'none' — block plugins
- frame-ancestors 'none' — prevent framing (clickjacking)
- base-uri 'self' — prevent base tag hijacking
- form-action 'self' — restrict form submissions

X-Content-Type-Options and X-Frame-Options were already present.

feat(score): add Agent Impact Score (0-100) per project

Computes a composite score measuring AI coding agent fingerprint:
- Throughput surge (0-25): lines/dev + PR rate growth vs baseline
- Slop signal (0-40): rejection rate + first-time contributor increase
- Review anomaly (0-20): TTM decrease while volume grows
- Consistency (0-15): how many months show the pattern

Score uses median of monthly values, requires 3+ months baseline.
Confidence levels: low/medium/high based on data availability.
Stored on Project model, recomputed after each sync.

Displayed as colored badge on project cards (small) and dashboard
header (large) with labels: Low/Moderate/High/Very High.

commit.message:
feat(SM-16): Restructure `resolveUncached` — replace `lookupFuzzy` data source for all tiers (#764)

* Initial plan

* chore: initial plan for SM-16 resolveUncached refactor

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/abhigyanpatwari/GitNexus/sessions/0f505332-25be-46a7-b78e-fde58c1fc6fd

Co-authored-by: magyargergo <11230420+magyargergo@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(SM-16): restructure resolveUncached — replace lookupFuzzy with targeted index lookups

- Remove single lookupFuzzy call that fed all Tier 2a/2b/3 in resolveUncached
- Tier 2a: iterate importedFiles with lookupExactAll per file (O(imports) × O(1))
- Tier 2b: iterate symbols.getFiles() filtered by isFileInPackageDir + lookupExactAll
  (O(files) × O(1), avoids global name scan)
- Tier 3: replace with lookupClassByName + lookupImplByName + lookupFuzzyCallable
  (three O(1) index lookups covering class-like, Rust impl blocks, and callables)
- Add getFiles() to SymbolTable interface (exposes fileIndex.keys() for Tier 2b)
- Add lookupImplByName() to SymbolTable — dedicated Rust Impl index kept separate
  from classByName to preserve correct heritage-map resolution
- Remove allDefs parameter from walkBindingChain; always use lookupExactAll directly
- Add 29 new unit tests covering SM-16 changes and per-language fixtures
- fuzzyCallCount in getStats() is now 0 for all resolve() calls (acceptance criterion)

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/abhigyanpatwari/GitNexus/sessions/0f505332-25be-46a7-b78e-fde58c1fc6fd

Co-authored-by: magyargergo <11230420+magyargergo@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(SM-16): clean up — readable Tier 3 if-else, correct doc comment, remove unused import

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/abhigyanpatwari/GitNexus/sessions/0f505332-25be-46a7-b78e-fde58c1fc6fd

Co-authored-by: magyargergo <11230420+magyargergo@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(SM-16): address all PR #764 review findings

1. Eager callableIndex — maintained on add() like classByName/implByName,
   removing the O(globalIndex) lazy rebuild on the Tier 3 hot path.

2. Tier 2b inverted index — packageDirSuffix→Set<filePath> built lazily on
   first Tier 2b hit. Changes O(allFiles×packages) per resolution to
   O(packages×filesInPackage).

3. Tier 3 type exclusion documented — TypeAlias, Const, Variable are
   intentionally not reachable at Tier 3. 4 negative/positive tests added.

4. Tier 3 allocation guard simplified — single spread replaces 4-way if-else.

5. getFiles() live iterator documented with safety contract.

6. Remaining lookupFuzzy callers in call-processor.ts documented in the
   Tier 3 comment block.

7. Tier 2b language fixtures — added Rust, Kotlin, PHP tests (3 new).

Also merges origin/main (SM-15 accumulator fixes).

* fix(SM-16): address Codex adversarial review — Tier 2b cache lifecycle + Macro/Delegate at Tier 3

1. Tier 2b packageDirIndex now invalidated in clearCache() and clear(),
   preventing stale snapshots when symbols/packages are added between
   chunk processing phases.

2. Macro (C/C++) and Delegate (C#) added to CALLABLE_TYPES in the eager
   callableIndex, restoring Tier 3 reachability for these call targets
   that the old lookupFuzzy returned.

* fix(SM-16): address ce:review findings — Tier 2b cache lifecycle + Tier 3 perf + test gaps

1. packageDirIndex no longer invalidated in clearCache() — the index
   persists across file boundaries since packageMap and symbols are
   append-only during the calls phase. Only clear() (pipeline reset)
   invalidates. Prevents O(files×dirs) rebuild per-file.

2. Tier 3 short-circuit: return null before spread when all three
   indexes are empty, avoiding allocation on the common miss path.

3. Add Macro (C/C++) and Delegate (C#) Tier 3 regression tests —
   the only newly-added CALLABLE_TYPES were completely untested.

4. Add packageDirIndex invalidation regression test — verifies clear()
   resets the index and newly-added symbols are visible.

* fix(SM-16): address final review — deduplicate NamedImportMap + doc fixes

1. NamedImportMap: removed duplicate definition from resolution-context.ts,
   now imported directly from import-processor.ts (no re-export needed —
   no consumers imported it from resolution-context).

2. packageDirIndex build cost documented accurately in comment.

3. fuzzyCallCount scope documented in test comment.

4. Tier 2a test suite: added comment about Go/Kotlin/PHP coverage.
